title: abies firma var. bifida
intro: Abies firma var. bifida, also known as the Japanese fir, is a variant of the Abies firma species native to Japan, characterized by its unique forked branches. The medicinal properties of Abies firma var. bifida lie in its essential oils, which contain compounds like borneol, bornyl acetate, and terpinen-4-ol, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. This plant has been traditionally used to relieve coughs, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Abies firma var. bifida can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
